ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Woe to those who give less [than due],
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Woe to the defrauders!

ayahs.textArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا ٱكۡتَالُواْ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ يَسۡتَوۡفُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Allatheena itha iktaloo AAala alnnasi yastawfoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Who, when they take a measure from people, take in full.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Those who take full measure ˹when they buy˺ from people,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But if they give by measure or by weight to them, they cause loss.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: but give less when they measure or weigh for buyers.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Do they not think that they will be resurrected
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do such people not think that they will be resurrected

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: For a tremendous Day -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: for a tremendous Day—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Day when mankind will stand before the Lord of the worlds?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: the Day ˹all˺ people will stand before the Lord of all worlds?

ayahs.textArabic: كـَلَّآ إِنَّ كِتَٰبَ ٱلۡفُجَّارِ لَفِي سِجِّينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kalla inna kitaba alfujjari lafee sijjeenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No! Indeed, the record of the wicked is in sijjeen.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But no! The wicked are certainly bound for Sijjîn ˹in the depths of Hell˺—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And what can make you know what is sijjeen?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and what will make you realize what Sijjîn is?—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: It is [their destination recorded in] a register inscribed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: a fate ˹already˺ sealed.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Woe, that Day, to the deniers,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Woe on that Day to the deniers—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Who deny the Day of Recompense.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: those who deny Judgment Day!

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا يُكَذِّبُ بِهِۦٓ إِلَّا كُلُّ مُعۡتَدٍ أَثِيمٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama yukaththibu bihi illa kullu muAAtadin atheemin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And none deny it except every sinful transgressor.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: None would deny it except every evildoing transgressor.

ayahs.textArabic: إِذَا تُتۡلَىٰ عَلَيۡهِ ءَايَٰتُنَا قَالَ أَسَٰطِيرُ ٱلۡأَوَّلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Itha tutla AAalayhi ayatuna qala asateeru alawwaleena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, "Legends of the former peoples."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Whenever Our revelations are recited to them, they say, “Ancient fables!”

ayahs.textArabic: كـَلَّاۖ بَلۡۜ رَانَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِم مَّا كَانُواْ يَكۡسِبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kalla bal rana AAala quloobihim ma kanoo yaksiboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No! Rather, the stain has covered their hearts of that which they were earning.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But no! In fact, their hearts have been stained by all ˹the evil˺ they used to commit!

ayahs.textArabic: كـَلَّآ إِنَّهُمۡ عَن رَّبِّهِمۡ يَوۡمَئِذٖ لَّمَحۡجُوبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kalla innahum AAan rabbihim yawmaithin lamahjooboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No! Indeed, from their Lord, that Day, they will be partitioned.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Undoubtedly, they will be sealed off from their Lord on that Day.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then indeed, they will [enter and] burn in Hellfire.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Moreover, they will surely burn in Hell,

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ يُقَالُ هَٰذَا ٱلَّذِي كُنتُم بِهِۦ تُكَذِّبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma yuqalu hatha allathee kuntum bihi tukaththiboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then it will be said [to them], "This is what you used to deny."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and then be told, “This is what you used to deny.”

ayahs.textArabic: كـَلَّآ إِنَّ كِتَٰبَ ٱلۡأَبۡرَارِ لَفِي عِلِّيِّينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kalla inna kitaba alabrari lafee AAilliyyeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No! Indeed, the record of the righteous is in 'illiyyun.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But no! The virtuous are certainly bound for ’Illiyûn ˹in elevated Gardens˺—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And what can make you know what is 'illiyyun?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and what will make you realize what ’Illiyûn is?—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: It is [their destination recorded in] a register inscribed
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: a fate ˹already˺ sealed,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Which is witnessed by those brought near [to Allah].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: witnessed by those nearest ˹to Allah˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, the righteous will be in pleasure
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely the virtuous will be in bliss,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: On adorned couches, observing.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹seated˺ on ˹canopied˺ couches, gazing around.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: You will recognize in their faces the radiance of pleasure.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: You will recognize on their faces the glow of delight.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will be given to drink [pure] wine [which was] sealed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will be given a drink of sealed, pure wine,

ayahs.textArabic: خِتَٰمُهُۥ مِسۡكٞۚ وَفِي ذَٰلِكَ فَلۡيَتَنَافَسِ ٱلۡمُتَنَٰفِسُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Khitamuhu miskun wafee thalika falyatanafasi almutanafisoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The last of it is musk. So for this let the competitors compete.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: whose last sip will smell like musk. So let whoever aspires to this strive ˹diligently˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And its mixture is of Tasneem,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And this drink’s flavour will come from Tasnîm—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: A spring from which those near [to Allah] drink.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: a spring from which those nearest ˹to Allah˺ will drink.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ أَجۡرَمُواْ كَانُواْ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ يَضۡحَكُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna allatheena ajramoo kanoo mina allatheena amanoo yadhakoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, those who committed crimes used to laugh at those who believed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, the wicked used to laugh at the believers,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when they passed by them, they would exchange derisive glances.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: wink to one another whenever they passed by,

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِذَا ٱنقَلَبُوٓاْ إِلَىٰٓ أَهۡلِهِمُ ٱنقَلَبُواْ فَكِهِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waitha inqalaboo ila ahlihimu inqalaboo fakiheena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when they returned to their people, they would return jesting.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and muse ˹over these exploits˺ upon returning to their own people.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِذَا رَأَوۡهُمۡ قَالُوٓاْ إِنَّ هَٰٓؤُلَآءِ لَضَآلُّونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waitha raawhum qaloo inna haolai ladalloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when they saw them, they would say, "Indeed, those are truly lost."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And when they saw the faithful, they would say, “These ˹people˺ are truly astray,”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But they had not been sent as guardians over them.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: even though they were not sent as keepers over the believers.

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱلۡيَوۡمَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ مِنَ ٱلۡكُفَّارِ يَضۡحَكُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faalyawma allatheena amanoo mina alkuffari yadhakoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So Today those who believed are laughing at the disbelievers,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But on that Day the believers will be laughing at the disbelievers,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: On adorned couches, observing.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: as they sit on ˹canopied˺ couches, looking on.

ayahs.textArabic: هَلۡ ثُوِّبَ ٱلۡكُفَّارُ مَا كَانُواْ يَفۡعَلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hal thuwwiba alkuffaru ma kanoo yafAAaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Have the disbelievers [not] been rewarded [this Day] for what they used to do?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹The believers will be asked,˺ “Have the disbelievers ˹not˺ been paid back for what they used to do?”
